reasoning: The Lean signature captures each piece of the informal statement precisely. "Distinct odd primes p, q" is encoded as `p.Prime ∧ p > 2`, `q.Prime ∧ q > 2`, and `p ≠ q` — since the only even prime is 2, `Prime ∧ > 2` is equivalent to "odd prime". The divisibility `p - 1 ∣ q - 1` is well-defined in ℕ since p, q > 2 ensures p-1, q-1 ≥ 2 with no truncation. The coprimality condition `n.gcd (p*q) = 1` matches `(n, pq) = 1`, and the conclusion `n^(q-1) ≡ 1 [MOD p*q]` via `Nat.ModEq` is the standard Mathlib encoding of n^(q-1) ≡ 1 (mod pq). n is properly universally quantified as an implicit binder. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and no conclusion is changed.
